examples.title: Dynamic Array Insert at End
examples.description: Pseudocode for inserting element at end with automatic resizing
examples.code:
PROCEDURE pushBack(array, element)
BEGIN
    IF array.size == array.capacity THEN
        newCapacity = array.capacity * 2
        newArray = ALLOCATE(newCapacity)
        FOR i = 0 to array.size - 1 DO
            newArray[i] = array.elements[i]
        END FOR
        array.elements = newArray
        array.capacity = newCapacity
    END IF
    array.elements[array.size] = element
    array.size = array.size + 1
END
examples.explanation: When array is full, new array of double capacity is allocated, all elements are copied, then new element is added. Amortized O(1) because resizing is infrequent.

examples.title: Dynamic Array Implementation in Python
examples.description: Complete implementation of dynamic array class with resize capability
examples.explanation: Python list uses similar implementation. The append operation automatically handles resizing. Uses doubling strategy for amortized O(1) insertions.

examples.title: Dynamic Array in C
examples.description: C implementation of dynamic array with malloc and realloc
examples.explanation: C implementation uses malloc for initial allocation and realloc for resizing. realloc efficiently copies memory when possible. shrinkToFit reduces memory when load factor drops below 0.25.
examples.timeComplexity: O(1) amortized for pushBack, O(n) for shrinkToFit

examples.title: Dynamic Array Using ArrayList in Java
examples.explanation: Java's ArrayList uses growth factor of 1.5 (50% increase). Default initial capacity is 10. Provides O(1) amortized add/remove at end, O(n) for insertions/removals at other positions.
examples.timeComplexity: O(1) amortized add/remove at end, O(1) random access, O(n) insertion at position
